rulings.Case Title: M. TODD JENKS VS. DLA PIPER RUDNICK GRAY CARY US LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Hearing On New Trial Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 21, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF M. JENKS' MOTION For A New Trial. Denied. Plaintiff has not established a basis for a new trial under CCP sec. 657. Plaintiff contends that the Court erred in finding that DLA Piper could enforce the arbitration provision in Plaintiff's offer letter. Plaintiff did not raise this argument in its opposition to Defendant's motion to compel arbitration. (Declaration of Christopher Scanlan, Ex. A, Jenk's Response to DLA Piper's Petition to Compel Arbitration.) Moreover, even if this argument had been raised, the Court would not have erred in finding that DLA Piper was entitled to enforce the agreement pursuant to Corp. Code sec. 16914(a). Plaintiff next contends that the Court erred in finding that his claims were within the scope of the arbitration agreement. Plaintiff argues that his claims arise under the Termination Agreement and 2006 STD Plan, which do not contain arbitration provisions. Plaintiff failed to provide a copy of these agreements as evidence in support of this motion. Further, the Termination Agreement has a limited integration clause and therefore does not supersede the arbitration provision in the employment offer. (See Cione v. Foresters Equity Servs., Inc. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 645, 637-38.) The arbitrator held that the 2006 STD Plan was not a contract. (Declaration of Christopher Scanlan, Ex. E, Arbitration Award, p. 6., "An STD claim could under certain circumstances be a contract. I hold that in this situation it was not.") Plaintiff contends that the Court erred in finding that it could not reconsider the arbitrability challenges raised in its opposition to the motion to confirm the arbitration award. "The court may not confirm an award without first finding the parties agreed in writing to arbitrate their dispute, unless a judicial determination of the issue has already been made (e.g., by a court considering a petition to compel arbitration)." (Toal v. Tardiff (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1208, 1220.) Because the Court determined the issue of arbitrability when it granted the motion to compel arbitration, the Court did not err in not reconsidering the issue in connection with confirming the arbitration award. (See CCP sec. 1008(e).) Plaintiff contends that the Court erred in concluding that the Court lacked jurisdiction to vacate or modify the arbitration award because Plaintiff failed to timely file a petition to vacate or modify the award. CCP sec. 1286 provides that "[i]f a petition or response under this chapter is duly served and filed, the court shall confirm the award as made, whether rendered in this state or another state, unless in accordance with this chapter it corrects the award and confirms it as corrected, vacates the award or dismisses the proceeding." Because there was no timely petition to vacate or modify the award, the mandatory language of CCP sec. 1286 required the Court to confirm the award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE SANCHEZ et al VS. JANESSA LEANNE TREVETHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Pltf To Provide Further Responses To Form Interoggs And Req For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 21, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT JANESSA TREVETHAN Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories and Document Request is granted. Defendant's motion was timely filed based on the date of service of the verifications. As to Form Interrogatories, all objections except attorney work product and attorney client privilege are overruled. As to the Document Request, all objections except attorney work product and attorney client privilege are overruled. Further responses shall be served within ten days of the date of this Order. Sanctions in the sum of $568.00 are awarded to defendant to be paid within fifteen days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TOM VS. AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summ Adjud / Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 21, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA AND UTAH INSURANCE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summ Adjudication. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Defendant has established that Plaintiff tendered the defense of the wrongful death action to Defendant and Defendant provided a defense in that case. (UMF #4.) Plaintiff may not rely on allegations in the Complaint to controvert material facts asserted by Defendant, and Plaintiff failed to produce any admissible evidence to controvert this fact. The statements cited in Plaintiff's declaration are inadmissible hearsay. Next, Defendant has also shown that the conveyance action was solely equitable in nature and that no damages for bodily injury or property damage were sought or could have been recoverable. (UMF #6.) Plaintiff's reliance on the request for "general and special damages" in the Complaint is not a material fact because such remedies are unavailable for the causes of action pled. Thus, Plaintiff has failed to controvert Defendant's evidence and create a triable issue of material fact. Defendant is also entitled to judgment in its favor for the sixth cause of action for breach of contract because Plaintiff failed to produce any admissible evidence establishing a triable issue as to Defendant's liability under the alleged agreements. Finally, because Plaintiff has produced no admissible evidence to support his claim that this breach was the result of Defendant's failure to provide an adequate defense in the wrongful death action, Plaintiff has failed to controvert Defendant's asserted material fact that the cause of action for breach of good faith was premised on the conveyance action. (UMF #10.) Because Defendant did not have a duty to defend Plaintiff in that action, Defendant necessarily cannot be liable for breach of good faith as alleged by Plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY INC. VS. R.F. MACDONALD CO., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 21, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY INC.'s Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. The court discloses that counsel for R.F. MacDonald previously worked for the court. The court believes that this prior employment is not a ground for disqualification. The court grants the motion to amend and plaintiff has leave to file the Second Amended Complaint. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment. The trial has been continued and the parties will have an opportunity to conduct discovery and defendants will have an opportunity to file motions for summary judgment and will not be prejudiced. This motion to amend is not the proper vehicle to resolve the economic loss and equitable tolling issues. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoenas To Eben Lee Kurtzman And Bradford Weston Iii And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against House Of Air, Llc And Daniel Crowley & Associates
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 21, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SEAN NICHOLSON, SHAWN NICHOLSON Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoenas To Eben Lee Kurtzman And Bradford Weston III And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against House Of Air, LLC And Daniel Crowley & Associates. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to modify deposition subpoenas directed to third parties is granted. The sole documentation to be produced shall be invoices and payment records with redaction of law-firm client(s) names and details of work performed. Also to be produced are written agreements between the plaintiff and the witnesses reflecting/referencing legal services to be performed. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $2,500.00 against defendant' counsel based on failure to timely "meet and confer" in good faith. Sanctions shall be paid within 20 days of the date of this Order.
